d: iPhone 16 Pro Max: 440x956pt (1320x2868px @3x). Largest current iPhone. Design in points, not pixels.
iPhone 16 Pro: 402x874pt (1206x2622px @3x). Pro model with Dynamic Island and ProMotion 120Hz display.
iPhone 16 Plus: 430x932pt (1290x2796px @3x). Large standard model.

t: iPhone 16 and iPhone 15 standard screen size
d: iPhone 16 and iPhone 15: 393x852pt (1179x2556px @3x). This is the PRIMARY design artboard size for modern iOS apps. Also matches iPhone 15 Pro and iPhone 14 Pro.

t: iPhone 15 Pro Max and 14 Pro Max screen size
d: iPhone 15 Pro Max and iPhone 14 Pro Max: 430x932pt (1290x2796px @3x). Same dimensions as iPhone 15 Plus and 16 Plus.

t: iPhone 14 Plus and iPhone 14 screen sizes
d: iPhone 14 Plus: 428x926pt (1284x2778px @3x). iPhone 14: 390x844pt (1170x2532px @3x). Notch models with slightly different dimensions from Pro models.
iPhone SE 3rd gen: 375x667pt (750x1334px @2x). Smallest current iPhone with Touch ID home button. No notch, no Dynamic Island, no home indicator. Status bar is 20pt.
iPad Pro 12.9-inch: 1024x1366pt. iPad Pro 11-inch: 834x1194pt. iPad Air: 820x1180pt. iPad mini: 744x1133pt. iPads use @2x scale factor. Design with split-view and multitasking in mind.

RULE: Design at 393x852pt (iPhone 15/16 standard) as the primary artboard. Always design in points (pt), not pixels. Points are resolution-independent and map to physical screen via scale factor (@2x or @3x). This ensures designs work across all device sizes.

t: Safe area insets for iPhone 16 Pro models
d: iPhone 16 Pro Max: top 62pt, bottom 34pt, left 0, right 0. iPhone 16 Pro: top 62pt, bottom 34pt. The top inset accounts for the Dynamic Island. Bottom inset accounts for the home indicator.

t: Safe area insets for iPhone 16/15/14 Pro
d: iPhone 16, 15, 15 Pro, 14 Pro: top 59pt, bottom 34pt. iPhone 14, 13, 12 notch models: top 47pt, bottom 34pt. iPhone SE: top 20pt (status bar only), bottom 0pt (no home indicator). iPad home indicator models: top 24pt, bottom 20pt.

RULE: NEVER place interactive elements (buttons, links, controls) in safe area insets. Content should respect safe areas but backgrounds and images can extend edge-to-edge behind status bar and home indicator. The safe area defines the region where all interactive and readable content must live.

Dynamic Island collapsed pill: approximately 126x37pt on iPhone 16 Pro. Expanded width varies by content, up to ~350pt. The minimum top safe area accounts for the Dynamic Island. Don't design custom Dynamic Island experiences unless specifically needed. Interactive content can expand from Dynamic Island via Live Activities.

Navigation bar with large title: 96pt total height (44pt standard bar + 52pt large title area below). Standard/collapsed navigation bar: 44pt height. Large title collapses to standard on scroll. Status bar above nav bar is approximately 54-62pt depending on device (includes Dynamic Island area on newer phones).

Tab bar: 49pt content height + 34pt home indicator area = 83pt total on Face ID devices. On iPhone SE (no home indicator): 49pt total. Maximum 5 tab items; use a 'More' tab for additional items. Tab bar icons are 25x25pt with label below. Tab bar sits at the very bottom of the screen.

t: Home indicator, toolbar, and search bar
d: Home indicator: 5pt tall, 134pt wide, 8pt from bottom edge, sits inside the 34pt bottom safe area. Toolbar: 44pt height, positioned above tab bar or home indicator. Search bar: 36pt height. Navigation bar always shows a back button when inside a navigation stack.

Minimum touch target: 44x44pt (Apple HIG requirement). Recommended: 48x48pt for primary actions. Minimum spacing between adjacent touch targets: 8pt. The visual element can be smaller than 44pt but the tappable area must always be at least 44x44pt. Thumb zone: bottom third of screen is easiest to reach — place primary actions there.

t: SF Pro type scale — Large Title through Title 3
d: Large Title: 34pt Regular, tracking 0.37. Title 1: 28pt Regular, tracking 0.36. Title 2: 22pt Regular, tracking 0.35. Title 3: 20pt Regular, tracking 0.38. These are the heading levels in the iOS type system. Use SF Pro Display for sizes >= 20pt.

t: SF Pro type scale — Headline and Body
d: Headline: 17pt Semibold, tracking -0.41. Body: 17pt Regular, tracking -0.41. Callout: 16pt Regular, tracking -0.32. These are the primary content text styles. Body is the default text style for most content. Use SF Pro Text for sizes < 20pt.

t: SF Pro type scale — Subheadline through Caption
d: Subheadline: 15pt Regular, tracking -0.24. Footnote: 13pt Regular, tracking -0.08. Caption 1: 12pt Regular, tracking 0. Caption 2: 11pt Regular, tracking 0.07. Minimum readable text is 11pt, minimum body text is 13pt.

t: SF Pro font families and Dynamic Type
d: SF Pro Display: use for text sizes >= 20pt. SF Pro Text: use for text sizes < 20pt. SF Pro Rounded: for playful or friendly UI styles. SF Mono: for code, technical content, or tabular numbers. RULE: Support Dynamic Type — users can change system text size in accessibility settings. All text must scale. Minimum readable text: 11pt, minimum body text: 13pt.

SF Symbols: 5000+ symbols built into iOS. Available weights: ultralight, thin, light, regular, medium, semibold, bold, heavy, black. Rendering modes: monochrome, hierarchical, palette, multicolor. Symbols scale with text size — use point size matching. RULE: Use SF Symbols for all system icons to maintain visual consistency with iOS. Symbols align with text baseline.

8pt grid: all spacing and sizing should use multiples of 8pt (8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56, 64...). 4pt baseline grid for vertical text rhythm (line heights in multiples of 4pt). Content margins: 16pt for compact width layouts (iPhone), 20pt for regular width layouts (iPad/landscape). Use 8pt grid for layout spacing, 4pt grid for fine-tuning alignment.

Section spacing: 24-35pt between content groups. List content inset: 16pt left padding for grouped list content. Standard cell padding: 16pt horizontal, 12pt vertical. These spacings create visual hierarchy and breathing room between content sections following Apple HIG patterns.

t: Standard corner radii for iOS components
d: Buttons: 12pt corner radius (standard). Cards: 16pt. Sheets and modals: 10pt. System alerts: 14pt. Search bar: 10pt. Full pill shape: height / 2. IMPORTANT: iOS uses continuous corner radius (squircle), not standard circular radius. In Figma, enable corner smoothing at approximately 60% to match the iOS squircle.

t: App icon and tab bar icon corner radius
d: App icon: rendered at 60pt on home screen with continuous (superellipse) corners. In Figma, use the official Apple icon mask template — do not try to manually create the radius. Tab bar icons have no corner radius. App icon full resolution: 1024x1024px for App Store. Always use the Apple-provided icon grid and mask.

iOS system colors: Blue #007AFF, Green #34C759, Indigo #5856D6, Orange #FF9500, Pink #FF2D55, Purple #AF52DE, Red #FF3B30, Teal #5AC8FA, Yellow #FFCC00. These colors are optimized for both light and dark mode. They have built-in dark mode variants with adjusted brightness and saturation.

Semantic colors adapt automatically to light/dark mode. Label hierarchy: primary, secondary, tertiary, quaternary. Fill hierarchy: primary through quaternary. Separator and link colors also adapt. Light backgrounds: #FFFFFF (primary), #F2F2F7 (secondary/grouped). Dark backgrounds: #000000 (primary), #1C1C1E (secondary), #2C2C2E (tertiary). RULE: Use semantic colors so they automatically adapt to appearance modes.

Grouped backgrounds are used for inset grouped table views (like Settings app). systemGroupedBackground: #F2F2F7 (light), #000000 (dark). secondarySystemGroupedBackground: #FFFFFF (light), #1C1C1E (dark). The grouped background pattern alternates: page background is gray, card/cell background is white (light mode). RULE: Always provide dark mode variants for any custom colors.

Dark mode backgrounds get darker, not inverted. Base is pure black #000000 on OLED displays for true black pixels-off power saving. Elevated surfaces are slightly lighter: #1C1C1E (secondary), #2C2C2E (tertiary). Elevation equals lighter shade in dark mode. Don't simply invert your light design — redesign for dark: reduce contrast, increase color vibrancy.

t: Dark mode text, colors, and shadows
d: Dark mode text: primary white #FFFFFF, secondary approximately rgba(255,255,255,0.6). Colors should be slightly desaturated and brighter for contrast on dark backgrounds. Shadows are nearly invisible in dark mode — rely on background elevation differences for depth instead. RULE: Test all custom colors for WCAG contrast ratio in both light and dark modes.

Bottom sheet: slides up from bottom, partially covers content behind. Sheet detents: .medium (approximately half screen), .large (full screen minus status bar), or custom height. Grabber handle at top: 36pt wide, 5pt tall, centered horizontally with 6pt top padding. Use bottom sheets for secondary actions and quick selections. Full modal for focused tasks that require explicit dismissal.

Modal presentations: fullScreen covers entire screen, pageSheet is inset from screen edges with rounded top corners (10pt radius). Popover: pointed callout anchored to a source element, primarily used on iPad. On iPhone, popovers typically fall back to sheet presentation. All modals can be dismissed by swiping down (interactive dismissal).

t: List and table view row heights
d: Standard row minimum height: 44pt. Subtitle row: 58-60pt. Large header row: 56pt. Section header: 32pt. Separator line: 0.5pt height, inset 16pt from left edge (or full width for plain style). These are minimum heights — rows expand to fit content with Dynamic Type.

Grouped/inset grouped style: use for settings screens and forms. Cards with rounded corners on a grouped background. Plain style: use for content lists like messages, files, contacts. Swipe actions: destructive actions use red, archive uses purple or blue, custom colors for other actions. RULE: Use inset grouped for settings-type screens, plain for content-type lists.

t: Pull to refresh and infinite scroll
d: Pull to refresh: standard UIRefreshControl spinner appears when pulling down on scrollable content. Trigger point is approximately 60-80pt pull distance. Infinite scroll: load more content at bottom with an activity indicator spinner. Show spinner when fetching, seamlessly append new items. Avoid pagination buttons — prefer infinite scroll for feeds.

Action sheet: slides up from bottom with a list of action options plus a cancel button separated by a gap. Actions are full-width buttons stacked vertically. Destructive actions shown in red. Alert: centered dialog box, maximum 2-3 buttons. Default/primary action uses bold text. Destructive actions use red text. Alert title is bold, message is regular weight below.

Context menu: triggered by long press, reveals a list of actions with an optional preview of the content above the menu. Items can have SF Symbol icons. Haptic feedback types: light (selection change), medium (impact/confirmation), heavy (notification/warning). Use haptics to reinforce important interactions. Toast/banner notifications appear at top of screen and auto-dismiss.

t: Figma artboard and grid setup for iOS
d: Primary artboard: 393x852pt for iPhone 15/16 standard. Grid: 4 columns, 16pt margins on each side, 8pt gutter between columns. This gives a usable content width of 361pt (393 - 32 margin). Set color mode to sRGB. Enable pixel snapping in Figma for crisp 1x rendering. Name frames with device names for clarity.

Export assets at three scales: @1x (points, for standard), @2x (retina, 2x points), @3x (super retina, 3x points). Name exports with @2x and @3x suffixes. Use PDF or SVG for vector icons. PNG for raster images. Use the official Apple Design Resources Figma iOS kit from Apple for accurate system components.

iOS navigation patterns: hierarchical (drill-down with back buttons in nav stack), flat (tab-based switching between top-level sections), content-driven (document or media browsing). Tab bar persists across all main sections. Navigation bar shows current location and back path. Use push navigation for drilling into content, modal for creating new items or focused tasks.

Thumb reach zones on iPhone: bottom third of screen is natural/easy reach (primary actions go here), middle third is comfortable stretch, top third is difficult to reach one-handed. iOS provides Reachability (double-tap home indicator to slide content down). Place the most frequently used actions in the bottom portion of the screen. Tab bars and toolbars leverage this zone.

RULE: Never use color as the only way to convey information — always pair with icons, text, or shape for accessibility. Use system tint color (blue by default) for interactive elements. Destructive actions always use system red. Maintain minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text, 3:1 for large text (WCAG AA). Use semantic color names in design tokens so dark mode works automatically.

t: SF Symbols sizing and custom symbols
d: SF Symbols match the x-height of text at the same point size, so a 17pt symbol pairs naturally with 17pt Body text. For standalone icon use, common sizes are 20-28pt. Custom symbols must be created using the SF Symbol template app and follow Apple's guidelines for alignment, margins, and weight variants. Always provide all 9 weight variants for custom symbols.

Search bar: 36pt height, sits below navigation bar or inline in content. Search becomes active with keyboard and cancel button. Show search suggestions as user types. Scope bar: segmented control below search bar to filter categories. Results appear in a table view below. Use token-based search for complex filters. Recent searches shown when search field is activated.

Onboarding: maximum 3-5 screens, swipeable pages with page indicator dots. Focus on benefits not features. Allow skip option. Each page: illustration at top, title, brief description. Empty states: show helpful illustration, explanatory text, and a primary call-to-action button. Never show a blank screen — always guide the user to next steps.

Standard keyboard height: approximately 291pt on iPhone (including suggestion bar). Keyboard with toolbar: add 44pt. When keyboard appears, content should scroll or resize to keep the active text field visible. Use inputAccessoryView for toolbars above keyboard. Dismiss keyboard on tap outside or scroll down. Avoid placing important actions behind the keyboard.

Status bar shows time, cellular signal, Wi-Fi, and battery. Height is part of the top safe area (included in the 47-62pt top inset). Two styles: default (dark text for light backgrounds) and light content (white text for dark backgrounds). Status bar is always visible except in full-screen media. Navigation bar automatically manages status bar style. Don't place content behind the status bar.

iOS uses spring-based animations with natural deceleration (not linear easing). Standard duration: 0.3-0.35s for transitions. Navigation push/pop: 0.35s horizontal slide. Modal present: 0.35s slide up with background dimming. Use reduce-motion accessibility setting — provide alternative non-animated transitions. Animations should be functional, not decorative. Interactive gestures use interruptible animations.

Support Dynamic Type for all text (scales with user preference from xSmall to AX5). Minimum touch target 44x44pt. Support VoiceOver: all elements need accessibility labels. Minimum contrast ratio 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components. Don't rely on color alone for meaning. Support Bold Text setting. Support Reduce Motion. Support Reduce Transparency. Test with VoiceOver and accessibility inspector.
